name: Chocolate Bread Pudding with Cinnamon Creme Anglaise-Clh
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease six 3 3/4-inch individual fluted tube pans or 8-oz timbale molds.
In the top of a double boiler, over simmering water, melt chocolate.
In a 2-quart saucepan, heat half-and-half and 1 cup sugar to boiling.
2. In a large bowl, with an electric mixer, beat eggs and remaining 1/2 cup
sugar until thick and lemon-colored. Slowly beat in the half-and-half
mixture. Stir in bread crumbs and melted chocolate just until combined,
forming pudding batter.
3. Heat oven to 400°F. Fill greased pans with batter. Bake puddings for
30 to 40 minutes, or until centers spring back when gently pressed with
a fingertip.
4. Prepare Cinnamon Creme Anglaise.
5. Cool puddings in pans on a wire rack for 10 minutes; unmold.
6. To serve, pour some Cinnamon Creme Anglaise onto each of six dessert plates.
Place Chocolate Bread Pudding on top of the Creme Anglaise.
Cinnamon Creme Anglaise:
In a 2-quart saucepan, heat 3 cups half-and-half to boiling.
In a large bowl, with a wire whisk, beat 4 large egg yolks and 6 tablespoons
sugar. Slowly beat the half-and-half into the yolk mixture; add 1 vanilla bean,
split, 1 cinnamon stick, and 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon. Pour all back
into the saucepan and cook, stirring constantly, over low heat until the Creme Anglaise
coats a spoon. Do not boil. Strain the Creme Anglaise into a bowl and refrigerate
until ready to serve.
